ideas.title: Route more idea-engine/self-improve calls to idle Spark GPUs
ideas.why: Both Spark GB10 GPUs report util 0 while router shows only 7 spark jobs today vs 56 cloud-hard — paid cloud tokens are being spent on work two idle local GPUs could do for free.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: In-browser Rust/WASM playground for blog-logic
ideas.why: The whole differentiator is a 100%-covered blog-logic crate, yet readers can't touch it — an embedded sandbox that runs the crate's pure fns live would make the 'dark factory' credible and shareable.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: Live in-browser terminal that talks to the brain
ideas.why: brain_state.json already tracks facts/nodes/synapses/mastery locally, but visitors can't query it — a Cmd-K command palette + REPL would turn the passive HUD into an interactive 'talk to the factory' surface.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: 3D WebGL knowledge graph of the brain's nodes/synapses
ideas.why: The brain already computes a node/synapse knowledge graph that's currently reduced to a 2D pulse — rendering it as a navigable force-directed 3D graph is the 'whoa' showpiece that visualizes what the factory actually knows.

ideas.title: MOONSHOT: Spark-backed AI 'ask the author' chat on the essays
ideas.why: Idle local GPUs (util 0) plus a fast-growing essay corpus is a wasted RAG opportunity — a chat that answers questions grounded in the blog's own posts turns the archive into a queryable knowledge base and drives dwell time.

ideas.title: Honor prefers-reduced-motion across all canvas/pulse animations
ideas.why: The site is saturated with always-on animated widgets (brain pulse, golden-hour, etc.) yet the signals show no reduced-motion handling — a real WCAG 2.3.3 gap that also burns battery on visitor devices.
